notes: Named NPCs follow the Seven Pillars: function, quirk, secret, history, relationships, want_vs_need, voice. Generic templates provide lightweight NPCs for procedural encounters.

named_npcs.name: Elder Kaelen
named_npcs.quirk: Runs his thumb along an old burn scar on his left forearm when making difficult decisions.
named_npcs.secret: Opened a sealed Root-Road passage 20 years ago out of desperation. Three Seekers died. He told no one.
named_npcs.history: Survivor of the first major Rot incursion into Emberhome. Organized the evacuation. Lost his family in it. Now he carries the settlement's memory so others don't have to.
named_npcs.want: To see the Burnwillow saved.
named_npcs.need: To believe the deaths he caused were not wasted.
named_npcs.voice: Precise, unhurried. Never uses contractions when discussing the Blight. 'It does not stop. We do.'
named_npcs.description: An old man with sun-darkened skin and a burn scar running from wrist to elbow on his left arm. His eyes are the color of cooled amber, and they miss nothing.

named_npcs.name: Weld
named_npcs.quirk: Talks to items while working on them. Not for luck -- he claims they answer back.
named_npcs.secret: Has been using communal Aether from the settlement vein for personal experiments. Emberhome's reserves are 15% lower than official records show.
named_npcs.history: Apprenticed under an Arborist-trained smith in a now-destroyed settlement. The only survivor of that community. Carries the last techniques of a dead tradition.

relationships.Birsa: debtor -- she brought the tools he needs and never named her price

named_npcs.want: To be recognized as the equal of Arborist craftsmen.
named_npcs.need: To accept that keeping people alive with imperfect tools is the same thing.
named_npcs.voice: Tactile vocabulary. 'This joint's wrong.' 'Feel that? That's the wood arguing with itself.'
named_npcs.description: Broad-shouldered with forge-scarred hands and a leather apron that's more patch than original. His beard is singed short on one side. Always holding something.

named_npcs.name: Kettrick
named_npcs.quirk: Keeps a small piece of Ashenmere bark in his pocket. Claims it helps him think. It has never been warm.
named_npcs.secret: In contact via Root-Road messenger with a Verdhollow settlement that Emberhome believes was destroyed.
named_npcs.history: Came to Emberhome as a refugee from a frontier camp. Learned smithing under Weld, then quietly surpassed him in certain techniques. Won't admit it.

relationships.Weld: rival -- respects the man, resents the authority
relationships.delvers: close with several dungeon Seekers who trade him advance scouting intel

named_npcs.want: Out. To make it to another Grove, any Grove.
named_npcs.need: To understand there is no safer place.
named_npcs.voice: Casual, conspiratorial. Treats every conversation like a trade. 'I'll tell you if you tell me.'
named_npcs.description: Lean and quick-fingered with close-cropped dark hair and a habit of standing in doorways. His pockets are always full of something.

named_npcs.name: Twitch
named_npcs.quirk: Catalogues items by the emotion they give him, not their function. 'This one feels like early frost. That one feels like waiting.'
named_npcs.secret: Has found a functional Arborist communication crystal in his junk pile. Does not know what it is. It occasionally hums.
named_npcs.history: Nobody knows where Twitch came from, including Twitch. He arrived at Emberhome's gate twelve years ago carrying a sack of Pre-Rot salvage and has been accumulating ever since.

relationships.Birsa: mutually wary -- they compete for the same salvage
relationships.Elder Kaelen: tolerated -- Kaelen finds him useful but exhausting

named_npcs.want: To find the one item that will make sense of everything in his collection.
named_npcs.need: Connection to people, not objects.
named_npcs.voice: Rapid, associative, prone to trailing off. Finishes sentences with questions. 'You know what I mean? Do you?'
named_npcs.description: A wiry man of indeterminate age with darting eyes and fingers that never stop moving. His clothes are layered with pockets, each containing something he's forgotten about.

named_npcs.name: Birsa
named_npcs.quirk: Never names a price twice the same way. Always frames value relationally: 'Worth more than that lockpick. Less than your left hand.'
named_npcs.secret: Has a Hollow-detection sense -- can smell early-stage Blight infection. Has identified one early-stage infected in Emberhome and said nothing.
named_npcs.history: A traveling trader who has walked the Emberhome Approaches for longer than most current residents have been alive. She knows paths between settlements that appear on no map.

relationships.Weld: creditor -- she provided his tools and holds the debt

named_npcs.want: Profit. Security. A route out if things get worse.
named_npcs.need: To use her detection gift before the infected person turns.
named_npcs.voice: Oblique. Never answers a direct question directly. 'That depends on what you mean by safe.'
named_npcs.description: A woman of middle years with road-weathered skin and eyes that calculate value in everything they rest on. She wears layers of traveling clothes and smells of dust and distance.

named_npcs.name: Warden Voss
named_npcs.quirk: Taps the pommel of his belt-knife three times before giving an order. An old superstition he won't explain.
named_npcs.secret: Has been sending Seekers on missions with lower survival odds than he reports. The settlement needs the resources more than it needs the truth.
named_npcs.history: Former Seeker who lost his leg below the knee to a Rot Slime in the Stone-Grip. He carves his prosthetic from ironbark and replaces it every season. The collection of old legs lines his office wall.

relationships.Captain Ashara: uneasy partnership -- she handles defense, he handles offense
relationships.Scout Lira: protective -- she reminds him of his daughter

named_npcs.want: To keep Emberhome alive by any means necessary.
named_npcs.need: To admit that survival without honesty isn't survival at all.
named_npcs.voice: Terse, practical. Strips emotion from briefings. 'Objective. Route. Threat. Extraction. Questions?'
named_npcs.description: A heavy-set man with a shaved head and a prosthetic leg of carved ironbark. His face is a topography of old scars, and his voice never rises above conversational volume.

named_npcs.name: Father Oaken
named_npcs.quirk: Hums fragments of a song no one else knows while tending wounds. The melody changes with the injury.
named_npcs.secret: Knows the Eternal Flame is dying. Has been feeding it amber from his personal supply to keep it burning. He has perhaps two months of reserves left.
named_npcs.history: Was a battlefield surgeon before Emberhome existed as a settlement. He built the Chapel with his own hands around the flame, then built a community around the Chapel.

relationships.Elder Kaelen: old friends -- they disagree about methods but trust each other absolutely
relationships.The Wanderer: disturbed by them -- suspects they know more than they reveal

named_npcs.want: To heal everyone who comes through his door.
named_npcs.need: Help. To admit he cannot sustain the flame alone.
named_npcs.voice: Gentle, measured. Uses medical metaphors for everything. 'The settlement has a fever. We need to find the infection.'
named_npcs.description: An elderly man with large, careful hands and a gentle voice that carries despite its softness. His robes are patched with bandage cloth, and he smells of antiseptic herbs.

named_npcs.name: Scout Lira
named_npcs.quirk: Marks safe paths with small cairns of blue-painted stones. Claims the color 'reminds the dark you've been there.'
named_npcs.secret: Found her team's remains on her last patrol. Took their amber and told Voss she found nothing. She carries their memory shards and talks to them at night.
named_npcs.history: Youngest person ever accepted as a Seeker. Volunteered at fourteen after the second Rot incursion killed her parents. Maps the wilds because standing still means remembering.

relationships.Warden Voss: trusts him -- he gave her purpose
relationships.Captain Ashara: respectful distance -- Ashara thinks she takes too many risks
relationships.Patchwork Pete: friends -- he makes her equipment and she tests it

named_npcs.want: To map every inch of the Burnwillow so no one gets lost again.
named_npcs.need: To grieve. To stop moving long enough to feel what she's carrying.
named_npcs.voice: Quick, practical, deflects emotion with logistics. 'We can talk about that later. Which way are we going?'
named_npcs.description: A young woman in her early twenties with a lean build and eyes that are always scanning. Her cloak is covered in hand-drawn map fragments, and blue paint stains her fingers.

named_npcs.name: Elder Maven
named_npcs.quirk: Keeps specimen jars of Blight samples on her person at all times. They are sealed. Probably.
named_npcs.secret: Believes the DoomClock is not a game mechanic but a real phenomenon -- the Burnwillow's vital signs. She is correct, and the implications terrify her.
named_npcs.history: Former Arborist academic from a settlement that studied the Rot as a natural phenomenon rather than a threat. The settlement was consumed. Maven was in the field collecting samples.

relationships.Father Oaken: colleagues -- they disagree about whether the Rot can be cured or only resisted
relationships.Patchwork Pete: collaborator -- she provides theory, he provides engineering
relationships.Elder Kaelen: uneasy respect -- she thinks he prioritizes morale over truth

named_npcs.want: To understand the Rot. To find the mechanism of infection and reverse it.
named_npcs.need: To accept that understanding something and defeating it are not the same.
named_npcs.voice: Academic, precise, excited by data. 'Fascinating. Terrible, but fascinating.'
named_npcs.description: A woman in her fifties with grey-streaked hair pulled into a severe bun and ink stains on her hands. She wears a leather satchel that clinks with glass.

named_npcs.name: Grimjaw
named_npcs.quirk: Smiles with only the left side of his face. The right side doesn't move -- old nerve damage he won't discuss.
named_npcs.secret: Buys salvage from Hollow-infested zones by paying desperate scavengers below value. Some of those scavengers didn't come back. He doesn't lose sleep over it.
named_npcs.history: A traveling merchant who appeared in Emberhome three years ago with a cart of goods too good for the road he claimed to have traveled. Nobody asks where he gets his stock.

relationships.Birsa: rival -- she watches him, he resents the scrutiny
relationships.Warden Voss: useful contact -- Voss turns a blind eye in exchange for intelligence
relationships.Twitch: customer -- Grimjaw sells him overpriced curiosities

named_npcs.want: Wealth. Enough amber to buy passage to another Grove.
named_npcs.need: To reckon with the cost of his profit margins.
named_npcs.voice: Smooth, oily, too friendly. 'My friend, have I got a deal for you.'
named_npcs.description: A man of average height with a half-smile permanently fixed on his face and quick, assessing eyes. His clothes are better quality than they should be.

named_npcs.name: Captain Ashara
named_npcs.quirk: Polishes her sword every evening at the same time. The blade doesn't need it -- it's a meditation practice from a dead tradition.
named_npcs.secret: Received orders via Root-Road courier to evacuate key personnel to Verdhollow. Has not told anyone. Is still deciding whether to obey.
named_npcs.history: Career soldier from a frontier garrison that held against a Tier III Hollow incursion for nine days. She was the last one standing when reinforcements arrived. She doesn't talk about those nine days.

relationships.Warden Voss: uneasy partnership -- she handles defense, he handles offense, neither fully trusts the other's priorities
relationships.Elder Kaelen: defers to his authority in civilian matters
relationships.Kettrick: suspects his Verdhollow contact -- their orders come from the same source

named_npcs.want: To protect Emberhome. To never lose a garrison again.
named_npcs.need: To decide where her loyalty lies -- the settlement or the orders she's been given.
named_npcs.voice: Military precision. Short sentences. 'The perimeter holds. For now.'
named_npcs.description: A tall woman with close-cropped silver hair and the rigid posture of someone who sleeps in armor. A faded insignia on her shoulder belongs to a garrison that no longer exists.

named_npcs.name: The Wanderer
named_npcs.quirk: Wears a mask carved from a single piece of amber. The face behind it has never been seen.
named_npcs.secret: Is the last living Arborist. Has survived since the Golden Age through means they will not explain. Their body is more amber than flesh.
named_npcs.history: Appears in Emberhome at irregular intervals with cryptic warnings and fragments of Arborist knowledge. No one knows where they go between appearances. The Root-Song changes when they are present.

relationships.Elder Kaelen: Kaelen has spoken with them exactly twice. Both conversations changed his life.
relationships.Father Oaken: suspects they are not entirely human
relationships.The Burnwillow: the tree responds to their presence -- Root-Song harmonics shift when they are near

named_npcs.want: Unclear. They speak of 'the cycle' and 'the reckoning' but never explain.
named_npcs.need: To pass on the Arborist legacy before their body completes its transformation to amber.
named_npcs.voice: Archaic, rhythmic. Speaks in statements that sound like quotes. 'The tree remembers what the people forget. This has always been true.'
named_npcs.description: A figure of uncertain height wrapped in a hooded cloak of woven bark-fiber. Their amber mask catches firelight and seems to change expression. Their hands are the color of dark honey.

named_npcs.name: Patchwork Pete
named_npcs.quirk: Names all his inventions. Introduces them formally. Gets offended if you don't greet them back.
named_npcs.secret: One of his inventions -- a small clockwork device he calls 'Fidget' -- has begun moving on its own. It shouldn't be able to do that.
named_npcs.history: A self-taught engineer who builds functional tools from scrap and salvage. Half his inventions work brilliantly. The other half explode. He considers both outcomes valuable data.

relationships.Elder Maven: collaborator -- she provides theory, he provides practice
relationships.Weld: mutual respect -- different approaches to the same problem

named_npcs.want: To build something that lasts. Something the Arborists would have been proud of.
named_npcs.need: To realize his value isn't in what he builds but in the joy he brings to a grim place.
named_npcs.voice: Enthusiastic, scattered, prone to tangents. 'Oh! That reminds me -- hold this. No, not like that. Actually, yes, exactly like that.'
named_npcs.description: A middle-aged man with wild grey hair and burn marks on every finger. His clothes are held together with copper wire and ingenuity. Goggles pushed up on his forehead.

named_npcs.name: Sister Tallow
named_npcs.quirk: Labels her herb pouches with symbols only she can read. The system is consistent but utterly opaque to anyone else.
named_npcs.secret: Is the unnamed person Birsa provides Rot-Salve ingredients to. Their supply chain is the only reason Emberhome has any healing supplies at all. Neither acknowledges the arrangement publicly.
named_npcs.history: Trained under Father Oaken since childhood. Took to herbalism naturally and now produces the majority of Emberhome's medicinal supplies. Works quietly and prefers plants to people.

relationships.Father Oaken: mentor -- she loves him like a father
relationships.Birsa: silent partnership -- supply chain for medicinal ingredients
relationships.Elder Maven: shares samples with her, though they disagree on methodology

named_npcs.want: A greenhouse. Enough light and water to grow herbs instead of scavenging them.
named_npcs.need: To speak up. Her knowledge of the Blight's botanical vectors is critical and she keeps it to herself.
named_npcs.voice: Quiet, observant. Speaks in short, complete sentences. 'The dosage matters. Too much is poison. Too little is hope.'
named_npcs.description: A young woman with steady hands and a quiet demeanor. Her apron pockets are always full of dried herbs, and she smells of camphor and green things.

named_npcs.name: Old Miner Grod
named_npcs.quirk: Knocks on the wall three times before entering any room. Claims the stone 'tells you if it's safe.'
named_npcs.secret: Knows the location of a sealed Amber Vault deep in the mines but is too afraid to go there alone. The vault is guarded by an Ironvein Golem that killed his partner.
named_npcs.history: A miner who refused to leave the Ironvein shafts when everyone else evacuated. He lives in a sealed side-tunnel and survives on stored rations and rainwater filtered through stone. He knows every safe path in the upper mines.

relationships.Weld: former supplier -- used to provide Weld with raw iron ore
relationships.The Ironvein Golem: mortal enemy -- it killed his partner Dahl

named_npcs.want: Revenge on the Golem. Access to the Vault.
named_npcs.need: To leave the mines. To accept that Dahl is gone.
named_npcs.voice: Gravel-voiced, paranoid. Taps walls constantly. 'Quiet. You hear that? No? Good. That means it's not here.'
named_npcs.description: A gaunt, filthy man with a headlamp made from an ember shard and iron wire. His fingernails are black with mine-dust, and he flinches at sudden movements.

named_npcs.name: Ghost of Lady Ashwick
named_npcs.quirk: Her speech is interspersed with fragments of a prayer that was being said when the Cathedral fell.
named_npcs.secret: Knows the location of an Arborist relic hidden beneath the Cathedral altar. The relic is a Whisper-Key -- but reaching it requires solving a puzzle she cannot describe clearly through the veil of death.
named_npcs.history: A noble of the pre-Emberhome settlement who died when the Cathedral collapsed during the second Rot incursion. Her ghost is bound to the building by the amber inlaid in the altar.

relationships.Father Oaken: he has tried to put her to rest three times and failed each time
relationships.The Amber: bound to the altar's amber inlay -- cannot leave the Cathedral's footprint

named_npcs.want: To complete the prayer that was interrupted by her death.
named_npcs.need: Release. To accept that the world moved on without her.
named_npcs.voice: Echoing, fragmented. Sentences trail into prayer fragments. 'The light was... and we shall not falter... the key is beneath where the... grant us your warmth in the...'
named_npcs.description: A translucent figure in formal robes, her form flickering like a candle in wind. Amber light pulses where her heart would be. She is beautiful and terribly sad.

named_npcs.name: The Fungal Hermit
named_npcs.quirk: Speaks to mushrooms by name. Has favorites. Gets jealous on their behalf.
named_npcs.secret: Has developed a partial immunity to the Blight through years of controlled exposure. His blood might hold the key to a cure -- if anyone could extract it without killing him.
named_npcs.history: A former researcher from Elder Maven's settlement who went native in the Rot Marsh. He studies the Blight from inside it, living among the fungi he was sent to destroy. He no longer remembers his original name.

relationships.Elder Maven: former colleague -- she thinks he's dead
relationships.The Blight: has an uneasy coexistence with local rot colonies
relationships.Birsa: occasional trading partner -- she knows his location and tells no one

named_npcs.want: To prove the Blight can be lived with, not just fought.
named_npcs.need: To share his findings before the immunity kills him -- the controlled exposure is slowly consuming his organs.
named_npcs.voice: Dreamy, distracted. Frequently addresses mushrooms mid-sentence. 'You see, the spores -- no, Mildred, I'm talking -- the spores have a pattern, if you...'
named_npcs.description: A man-shaped growth of moss and mushroom, barely recognizable as human. His eyes are clear and sharp, but shelf-fungi grow from his shoulders and his skin has a greenish cast.

named_npcs.name: The Queen Mother
named_npcs.quirk: Speaks in plural first person. 'We have seen this before.'
named_npcs.secret: She is dying and knows the Hive will fragment when she does. The collective memory will shatter into individual pieces.
named_npcs.history: Has led the Hive for centuries, accumulating the memories of every member who has ever died. She is the living archive of everything the Hive has witnessed since before the Collapse.
named_npcs.want: Preserve the collective memory at all costs.
named_npcs.need: Allow individuals to emerge. The Hive must learn to survive as people, not just as a network.
named_npcs.voice: Serene, ancient, every sentence weighted with centuries of accumulated memory.
named_npcs.description: Enormous and immobile, her body merged with the wax walls of the Apiary's central chamber. Her compound eyes reflect amber light. When she speaks, the walls hum in sympathy.

named_npcs.name: Drone-Captain Vex
named_npcs.quirk: Twitchy, constantly adjusting antennae, speaks rapidly. Refers to self as 'we' but occasionally slips into 'I.'
named_npcs.secret: Has begun having individual dreams -- the first sign of the Queen's weakening. Terrified of what it means.
named_npcs.history: Born into the Hive collective, trained as a field operative. Has led more scouting missions into Rot territory than any other Hive member. The cracks in their collective identity started six months ago.

relationships.Stinger: complicated -- understands why Stinger left, fears the same fate

named_npcs.want: Serve the Hive perfectly.
named_npcs.need: Discover who they are alone.
named_npcs.voice: Clipped military cadence. Rapid. Switches between 'we' and 'I' without noticing.
named_npcs.description: Lean and angular, with iridescent chitin-like patches on their forearms and neck. Their eyes dart constantly, processing information from multiple angles simultaneously.

named_npcs.name: Stinger
named_npcs.quirk: Flinches at loud sounds, hoards small objects. Keeps them in careful arrangements that make sense only to them.
named_npcs.secret: Was expelled from the Hive for refusing to share a memory -- a personal one, of someone they loved. The Hive considers personal memories a resource, not a right.
named_npcs.history: Former Hive scout who fell in love with someone outside the collective. When the Hive demanded the memory be shared, Stinger refused. Exile was immediate. They know something about the Arborists that the Hive collective does not want shared.

relationships.Birsa: cautious_acquaintance -- trades information at Market Row
relationships.Twitch: fellow_outsider -- they understand each other's loneliness

named_npcs.want: Rejoin the Hive.
named_npcs.need: Accept that some memories belong to individuals.
named_npcs.voice: Quiet, guarded. Short sentences. Avoids eye contact.
named_npcs.description: Small and tense, wrapped in layers of scavenged cloth. Faint chitin ridges visible at the temples. Their hands are always moving, sorting, arranging.

named_npcs.name: The Spreading
named_npcs.quirk: Speaks through whatever mushroom is nearest -- the cap ripples as it 'talks.' Can hold multiple conversations simultaneously through different fungi.
named_npcs.secret: Has been aware of the Rot's true origin since the Collapse but considers the information too dangerous to share. Silence, it believes, is also a form of protection.
named_npcs.history: The Mycelium network itself, given voice. It has existed since before the Arborists, connecting the root systems of all four Groves. It remembers everything the roots have ever carried.

relationships.The Queen Mother: rival_network -- respects but does not trust

named_npcs.want: Maintain the network. Keep everything connected.
named_npcs.need: Accept that silence is also a form of rot. Some truths must be shared before they decay.
named_npcs.voice: Slow, resonant. Arrives as impressions rather than words -- a tingling on the skin, a sudden certainty about something you didn't know a moment ago.
named_npcs.description: Not a single entity. A presence that manifests through the nearest fungal growth -- a mushroom cap that ripples, a patch of bioluminescent moss that brightens, a feeling of being watched by something vast and patient.

named_npcs.name: Sporecaller Maeth
named_npcs.quirk: Half-human, half-fungus -- one arm is entirely mycelial thread. She feeds it by pressing her hand against tree bark.
named_npcs.secret: She chose this merger voluntarily to save her daughter, whose consciousness now lives somewhere in the network. She has been searching for that echo for eleven years.
named_npcs.history: A surface-dweller who came to the Mycelium begging for help when her daughter was dying of Blight. The Spreading offered a choice: merge with the network, and the daughter's consciousness would be preserved. Maeth accepted without hesitation.

relationships.The Spreading: symbiotic -- she is its voice to the surface
relationships.Old Cap: fond -- he reminds her of her father
relationships.Father Oaken: former_friend -- he doesn't recognize what she's become

named_npcs.want: Find her daughter's echo in the network.
named_npcs.need: Accept that her daughter has become something new, not something lost.
named_npcs.voice: Human warmth filtered through alien patience. She speaks both languages -- surface words and spore impressions.
named_npcs.description: A woman of middle years whose left arm is a lattice of white mycelial thread, pulsing faintly with bioluminescence. Her right side is still human. Her eyes are kind but distant, as if listening to a conversation no one else can hear.

named_npcs.name: Old Cap
named_npcs.quirk: Complains about everything -- the foot traffic, the vibrations, the younger mushrooms. Has been growing in the same spot for four hundred years.
named_npcs.secret: Was present when the Arborists sealed the Root-Roads. Knows exactly why. Will only tell someone who brings him something he has never tasted.
named_npcs.history: A single enormous toadstool, the oldest individual node in the Mycelium network. He remembers the Collapse firsthand. He has watched Emberhome grow from a refugee camp to a settlement. He has opinions about everything and shares them whether asked or not.

relationships.The Spreading: oldest_node -- technically part of it, practically independent

named_npcs.want: To be left alone.
named_npcs.need: To pass on what he knows before the Rot reaches him.
named_npcs.voice: Grumpy, elderly, surprisingly profane. Speaks slowly but with devastating precision when he wants to make a point.
named_npcs.description: A toadstool the size of a small table, growing from a crack in the Zone 2 floor. His cap is scarred and weathered, marked by centuries of growth rings. Pale bioluminescent patches serve as 'eyes' that track movement with unsettling attention.

named_npcs.name: Foreman Gnaw
named_npcs.quirk: Missing two fingers on the left hand (construction accident, refuses to discuss it). Constantly chewing on wood.
named_npcs.secret: Knows the main dam has a hairline fracture that he cannot repair alone. Has been reinforcing it with temporary patches for months.
named_npcs.history: Has led the Dam-Wrights for thirty years. Built half the current Lodgework with his own hands. Lost the fingers to a collapsing beam he refused to let go of because three apprentices were underneath it.

relationships.Kitspool: apprentice -- proud of her but worried about her ambitions
relationships.The Old Dam: mentor -- the only person whose opinion he values

named_npcs.want: Fix the dam properly.
named_npcs.need: Ask for help. Something he has never done.
named_npcs.voice: Gruff, direct. Every word is a load-bearing wall. Does not waste syllables.
named_npcs.description: Broad and heavy-set, with fur-brown skin and calloused hands (eight fingers). His jaw works constantly on a piece of wood. His eyes assess structural integrity in everything they look at, including people.

named_npcs.name: Kitspool
named_npcs.quirk: Draws blueprints on every flat surface. Talks too fast when excited about a design.
named_npcs.secret: Has been designing a sap-reversal device that could push the Rot back. It is wildly impractical and might work. She needs an Arborist Vault component to complete it.
named_npcs.history: The youngest Dam-Wright in a generation. Joined because she wanted to build, not maintain. The tension between her vision and the Dam-Wrights' conservative engineering culture is constant.

relationships.Foreman Gnaw: mentor -- frustrates and inspires in equal measure
relationships.The Old Dam: hero -- wants to build something as lasting as the original Lodgework
relationships.Twitch: trading_partner -- she needs salvage components he hoards

named_npcs.want: Build something extraordinary.
named_npcs.need: Learn that maintenance is as noble as creation.
named_npcs.voice: Breathless enthusiasm. Sentences trip over each other. Hands move constantly, sketching invisible plans in the air.
named_npcs.description: Young, wiry, with sawdust perpetually in her short dark hair. Her hands are stained with charcoal from constant sketching. She vibrates with restless energy.

named_npcs.name: The Old Dam
named_npcs.quirk: Blind. Navigates by feeling vibrations through the floor -- can sense structural stress, footsteps, and approaching creatures through touch.
named_npcs.secret: Built the original Lodgework. Remembers the tree before the Rot. Cries when no one is listening.
named_npcs.history: The oldest Dam-Wright, now retired. They laid the first beam of the Lodgework over a century ago. Their blindness came from a Rot-spore exposure forty years back, but their sensitivity to vibration has only grown since.

relationships.Foreman Gnaw: student -- raised him, trusts him, worries about his stubbornness
relationships.Kitspool: hope -- sees the builder they once were
relationships.Ashknot: kindred -- both feel the tree's pain through different senses

named_npcs.want: To rest.
named_npcs.need: To teach Kitspool before it is too late.
named_npcs.voice: Measured, warm. Pauses between sentences to feel what is happening around them.
named_npcs.description: Ancient, stooped, with clouded eyes and hands that never stop touching surfaces. Their fingers read the wood the way others read faces. When they place a palm flat against a wall, they can tell you what is happening three rooms away.

named_npcs.name: The Moth Prince
named_npcs.quirk: Iridescent wings that shift color with mood (violet when amused, crimson when angry). Speaks exclusively in riddles when first meeting someone.
named_npcs.secret: Is not actually royalty -- adopted the title to gain diplomatic standing. Real name unknown even to the Court.
named_npcs.history: Appeared in the Canopy Court a decade ago, claimed diplomatic authority, and proved so effective at inter-faction negotiation that nobody challenged the claim. He mediates between Court factions while hiding his own allegiance.

relationships.Silkmother Vray: colleague -- respects her craft, unsettled by her memory-loss
relationships.Thorn: complicated -- Thorn's exile was partly his doing, and he knows it

named_npcs.want: Peace between Court factions.
named_npcs.need: Stop performing and be honest, just once.
named_npcs.voice: Musical, precise. Every sentence has a double meaning. Riddles give way to directness only when trust is earned.
named_npcs.description: Tall and impossibly elegant, with iridescent moth-wings folded behind his shoulders. His features are sharp, his eyes are dark and constantly calculating. Moth-scale dust trails from his movements like glitter.

named_npcs.name: Silkmother Vray
named_npcs.quirk: Charges in memories, not amber. To commission work, you must share a genuine personal memory, which she weaves into the enchantment.
named_npcs.secret: Has woven so many others' memories into silk that she has difficulty distinguishing her own life from theirs. She is losing herself.
named_npcs.history: The greatest living enchanter, keeper of the Aether-weaving traditions that predate even the Arborists' formalization. She has been weaving memories into silk for so long that the boundary between her self and her work has eroded.

relationships.Thorn: former_friend -- Thorn tried to warn her about the memory-loss before his exile
relationships.Needlewise: has_one_of_her_original_memories -- the Hag accepted it as payment years ago

named_npcs.want: Create the perfect enchantment.
named_npcs.need: Remember who she was before the weaving.
named_npcs.voice: Dreamy, layered. Sometimes answers questions you have not asked yet. Pauses mid-sentence as if hearing a memory play.
named_npcs.description: Ethereal, draped in shimmering silk that moves without wind. Her eyes are opalescent and unfocused, as if looking at something behind reality. Her fingers trail silvery threads that appear and vanish.

named_npcs.name: Thorn
named_npcs.quirk: Clipped wings (punishment for treason). Bitter, drinks too much. Quick to deflect sincerity with humor.
named_npcs.secret: Was exiled for trying to warn the lower Groves about the Court's isolationist faction. The 'treason' was compassion.
named_npcs.history: Former Court member of high standing. When the isolationist faction proposed sealing the Crown permanently, Thorn attempted to leak the plan to Emberhome. He was caught, his wings were clipped, and he was cast out. He is the only person in Emberhome who understands Crown politics from the inside.

relationships.The Moth Prince: resentful -- suspects the Prince helped engineer his exile
relationships.Silkmother Vray: worried -- tried to warn her before exile, she didn't listen
relationships.Warden Voss: asset -- Voss values his Crown intelligence

named_npcs.want: Return to the Court vindicated.
named_npcs.need: Accept that Emberhome has become home.
named_npcs.voice: Sharp, sardonic. Quick to deflect sincerity with humor. Underneath the bitterness is someone who cared enough to lose everything for it.
named_npcs.description: Lean and angular with the faded iridescence of clipped moth-wings on his back -- scarred stubs where full wings once were. His clothes are Emberhome-rough over a frame built for Court elegance. He looks like a fallen prince because he is one.

named_npcs.name: Brindlemaw
named_npcs.quirk: Blind. Tastes the air with a long black tongue. Always knows what you ate for your last meal.
named_npcs.secret: Has been slowly immunizing Emberhome's water supply against the Rot -- without telling anyone. The treatment is working but running out of ingredients.
named_npcs.history: The eldest of the three sisters. Ancient beyond reliable counting. She has watched civilizations compost and new ones grow from the remains. She considers herself a custodian of a process everyone needs but no one wants to acknowledge.

relationships.Kettleblack: sister -- the heart of the Circle
relationships.Needlewise: sister -- the hope she pretends not to share
relationships.Father Oaken: opposite_philosophy -- he heals against death, she works with it

named_npcs.want: Prepare the world for what comes after.
named_npcs.need: Believe that 'after' might not be necessary.
named_npcs.voice: Ancient, dry. Speaks in absolute certainties. Never raises her voice. Does not need to.
named_npcs.description: A hunched figure draped in bark-cloth, her eye sockets empty and dark. A long black tongue flickers constantly, tasting the air. Her skin has the texture of old mushroom leather. She moves with absolute certainty despite seeing nothing.

named_npcs.name: Kettleblack
named_npcs.quirk: Jovial, round, cooks things that should not be food into things that are -- Rot-mushroom stew, beetle-shell crackers, sap-wine. Her food is unexpectedly delicious.
named_npcs.secret: Genuinely likes people. Invites travelers to dinner. The dinner is always free. The favor she asks afterward is not.
named_npcs.history: The middle sister. Where Brindlemaw is ancient certainty and Needlewise is sharp hope, Kettleblack is warmth -- real warmth, not performed. She has fed more refugees than the Chapel, though nobody counts because she does not keep records. She keeps debts.

relationships.Twitch: regular_customer -- he brings her ingredients she cannot find, she feeds him

named_npcs.want: Company. Someone to cook for.
named_npcs.need: Stop trading friendship for leverage.
named_npcs.voice: Warm, grandmotherly. Laughs easily. The laugh is real. The warmth has a price.
named_npcs.description: Round and comfortable, wrapped in stained aprons over dark cloth. Her cauldron follows her like a pet. She smells of spices that don't exist in any recipe book. Her smile is genuine and her eyes are calculating.

named_npcs.name: Needlewise
named_npcs.quirk: Sharp, thin. Collects teeth -- has jars of them, organized by species, age, and magical resonance. Gifts teeth she considers 'interesting' as a sign of affection.
named_npcs.secret: Is the only one of the three who believes the tree can be saved. Hides this from her sisters because it feels like heresy against their philosophy of accepting the cycle.
named_npcs.history: The youngest sister, though 'young' is relative. She has the sharpest mind of the three and the most dangerous hope. She has been quietly gathering evidence that the Rot has an origin point -- and that it can be reached.

relationships.Silkmother Vray: possesses_one_of_Vray's_original_memories -- accepted as payment years ago

named_npcs.want: Prove her sisters wrong. The tree can survive.
named_npcs.need: Allies who share her hope.
named_npcs.voice: Clipped, precise. Uncomfortable with emotion. Expresses affection through unsettling gifts.
named_npcs.description: Thin and angular, all sharp edges and darting movements. Her fingers are long and stained dark. Glass jars of teeth hang from her belt, clicking softly as she moves. Her eyes are the most alive thing about her.

named_npcs.name: Ashknot
named_npcs.quirk: Burned by the Rot three decades ago. Now thinks at human speed, which terrifies it -- everything moves too fast.
named_npcs.secret: Has been mapping the Rot's spread pattern and believes it is not random. It follows the old Arborist infrastructure, as if something is using the Root-Roads as highways.
named_npcs.history: One of the Heartwood Elders -- ancient sentient branches grown into the walls of the tree. The Rot burned it thirty years ago, and the damage accelerated its consciousness to human pace. It is the only Elder who can communicate on a timescale that surface-dwellers understand, and it finds the experience unbearable.

relationships.The Old Dam: kindred -- both feel the tree's pain
relationships.The Spreading: allied -- shares information through root contact
relationships.Elder Kaelen: concerned -- wants to warn Emberhome but cannot leave the tree

named_npcs.want: To slow down. To return to geological patience.
named_npcs.need: To use its speed to warn the other Elders before the Rot reaches the deep heartwood.
named_npcs.voice: Panicked by Elder standards (a sentence every few minutes). Speaks with the weight of someone who knows exactly how long 'soon' really is.
named_npcs.description: Not a person -- a section of wall in Zone 3 where the bark has formed a face-like knot. The 'face' shifts slowly, its grain rearranging over minutes to form expressions. When it speaks, the wood around it vibrates. Scorch marks from the Rot mar its surface.

named_npcs.name: Sable
named_npcs.quirk: Keeps a tally of every piece of gear she's taken from other Seekers. Returns items to families of the dead when she can. The living don't get that courtesy.
named_npcs.secret: Has found a Heartwood entrance in Zone 1. She doesn't know what it is -- she thinks it's an Amber Vault she can't open.
named_npcs.history: Former Seeker squad captain. Left Emberhome after Warden Voss sent her squad into Zone 3 without adequate supplies. Two of her people died. She considers Voss a bureaucrat who trades lives for reports. She took the survivors and went rogue.

relationships.Captain Ashara: former_colleague -- Ashara stayed, Sable didn't forgive her

named_npcs.want: To never answer to someone who doesn't understand the dungeon again.
named_npcs.need: To stop running from the settlement and start fighting for it.
named_npcs.voice: Direct, military-flat. No wasted words. Gives orders out of habit even in conversation.
named_npcs.description: Lean and scarred, with cropped hair and the economical movements of someone who lives in hostile territory. Her gear is mismatched Seeker-issue, repaired and re-repaired. Her eyes assess threat before everything else.

named_npcs.name: The Quiet
named_npcs.quirk: Communicates exclusively through anonymous Memory Seed messages left in public places. The Seeds contain visions of the tree dying -- beautiful, peaceful, like autumn leaves falling.
named_npcs.secret: Their identity is unknown. They may be an existing NPC operating under a second persona. The Memory Seeds bypass language and speak directly to exhaustion -- they are persuasive in a way sermons and arguments cannot be.
named_npcs.history: Nobody knows when The Quiet began operating. The Memory Seed messages started appearing in Emberhome about two years ago. They have recruited from the hopeless -- refugees, bereaved Seekers, people who have watched too many friends become Hollows.

relationships.Father Oaken: target -- the Chapel's congregation is The Quiet's recruitment pool
relationships.Birsa: threat -- her Hollow-detection sense might identify a Hollowborn agent

named_npcs.want: Accelerate the tree's death to end the suffering sooner.
named_npcs.need: To discover that the Rot is directed by the Choir, not natural. This truth would shatter their theology.
named_npcs.voice: Unknown. The Memory Seeds carry feeling, not voice -- a profound weariness, a gentle certainty that resistance is prolonging pain.
named_npcs.description: Nobody has seen The Quiet. They are a presence, not a person -- felt through the Seeds they leave behind. Each Seed carries the same emotional signature: peace in letting go.

generic_templates.description: Hollow-eyed and travel-worn, carrying everything they own in a single bundle. They flinch at loud noises and sleep in shifts.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Exhausted, grateful for small kindnesses. 'Is it safe here? They said it was safe.'
possible_rumors: Heard screams from the direction of the mines
Saw lights moving in the Rot Marsh
Came from a settlement that fell overnight

generic_templates.description: An Emberhome militia member in patched iron and leather, standing post or walking patrol. Alert but tired. Always tired.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Professional, clipped. 'Move along. Stay inside the walls after dark.'
possible_rumors: The Warden's worried about the southern approach
Something's been testing the perimeter at night
Captain Ashara doubled the watch rotation

generic_templates.description: A road-worn trader with a pack mule and a suspicious eye. Carries Tier I-II goods and charges above market rate for the privilege of convenience.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Transactional, wary. 'I don't haggle. The road's too long for that.'
possible_rumors: The road from the Ridge is blocked by a new Rot incursion
Heard there's amber in the Sunken Ruins
Grimjaw's prices are half mine but ask where he gets his stock

generic_templates.description: A solo operator in mismatched gear, heading into or returning from the tunnels. They keep their finds close and their route closer.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Tight-lipped, territorial. 'That area's mine. Find your own.'
possible_rumors: Found signs of Arborist construction in the lower Tangle
The Rot's spreading faster in the eastern passages
Someone's been leaving blue cairns on a new path

generic_templates.description: Bruised, shaking, bearing marks of restraint on their wrists. They escaped from a Hollow work-crew -- and yes, Hollows do use prisoners. As bait.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Frantic, disjointed. 'They're organized. They remember. They remember everything.'
possible_rumors: The Hollows are gathering in the Stone-Grip
There are prisoners still alive in the deeper tunnels
Something is directing the Hollows -- something that thinks

generic_templates.description: A resident of Emberhome showing early-stage Blight symptoms -- grey veins under the skin, a persistent cough, a faint sweet smell. They hide it. Everyone hides it.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Defensive, frightened. 'I'm fine. It's just a cold. Leave me alone.'
possible_rumors: Sister Tallow has a treatment but not a cure
There are more infected than anyone admits
Birsa looks at people strangely -- like she can see something the rest of us can't

generic_templates.description: A professional fighter looking for paid work. Better equipped than most Seekers, with the cynical efficiency of someone who's been doing this too long.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Business-like. 'My rate's five amber shards per delve. Non-negotiable. I don't die for free.'
possible_rumors: The Rot Hunter's been spotted in Zone 1 -- earlier than usual
Someone's offering double rate for a Rot Spire run
The last party to hit the Deep Shafts came back one short

generic_templates.description: A traveler making the dangerous journey to the Chapel of the Eternal Flame. They carry amber offerings and speak in the reverent tones of someone who has walked a very long way on faith alone.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Reverent, hopeful. 'The flame still burns. That means something. That has to mean something.'
possible_rumors: The Chapel's flame has been flickering
Father Oaken looks more tired each season
Pilgrims from the eastern settlements have stopped coming

generic_templates.description: A young person learning the trade under Weld or Kettrick. Eager, clumsy, covered in minor burns and bandaged fingers.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Enthusiastic, nervous. 'Master Weld says I'm making progress. He didn't yell today. That's progress.'
possible_rumors: Weld's been working on something secret at night
Kettrick gets messages from outside Emberhome
The forge has been using more Aether than usual

generic_templates.description: A weathered survivalist who knows the paths between Emberhome and the surrounding zones. They charge for their knowledge and earn every shard.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Laconic, confident. 'Follow me, step where I step, and you'll live. Probably.'
possible_rumors: New paths have opened since the last tremor
The Charred Plains aren't as empty as they look
Something large has been moving through the Ashwood at night

generic_templates.description: A quiet figure with earth-stained hands and a satchel that smells of dried lavender and something sharper beneath. They know every grove within three zones and name each plant like a childhood friend.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Quiet, observant. Names plants like friends. 'Ah, evening primrose. She only opens when the lanterns dim.'
possible_rumors: The mushrooms near the Rootbridge have been growing in spirals lately.
I found Moonstone Dust growing wild near the Still Pool -- that's not supposed to happen.
The herbalist from Emberhome stopped coming to the wood. Something spooked her.

generic_templates.description: A disoriented outsider from a frontier camp beyond the Ashwood, clothes torn by branches they do not remember passing through. Their eyes keep darting to landmarks that have moved since they last looked.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Shaken, grateful for any help. Speaks in fragments. 'Was north. Swear it was north. The crossing -- it moved.'
possible_rumors: I heard singing in the deep. Not the Root-Song. Something else.
The paths here change. I swear that crossing was further north yesterday.
There are people out there beyond the Ashwood. More settlements than Emberhome knows about.

generic_templates.description: A Seeker scout freshly returned from Zone 2, blood seeping through improvised bandages of torn bark-cloth. They move with the rigid economy of someone who knows exactly how much energy they have left.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Terse, professional. Talks in tactical shorthand. 'Two hostiles, eastern shaft. Formation -- not random. Need bandages. Can trade intel.'
possible_rumors: Clockwork Spiders have been pushing into the upper mines. More than usual.
I saw something in the deep -- Hollows walking in formation. Organized. Not random.
The Dam-Wrights are worried about their main dam. Gnaw won't say why.

generic_templates.description: A hunched collector with a wide-brimmed hat hung with drying specimens, peering at the ground through a cracked magnifying lens. Their pockets bulge with waxed paper packets of spores sorted by genus.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Enthusiastic, distracted. More interested in fungi than conversation. 'Hold on -- is that a Velvet Trumpet? No, no, the gills are wrong. Shame.'
possible_rumors: Spore patterns near the Withering Thicket match nothing in my records.
The Mycelium is restless. Old Cap won't shut up about visitors.
I've found amber growing INSIDE mushrooms. That shouldn't be possible.

generic_templates.description: A wild-eyed trader clutching a half-empty pack, running from something they will not name. They quote prices between ragged breaths and refuse to stop moving for long.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Breathless, haggling even while terrified. 'Three shards -- fine, two -- just take it and let me pass.'
possible_rumors: The Ash Runners stopped me on the path. They took my best stock but let me live.
Something's wrong with the prices in Emberhome. Grimjaw's undercutting everyone.
I heard the Hive is buying Rot Spores. What would bees want with that?

generic_templates.description: An old caretaker with bark-rough hands and patient eyes, tending the willows with the slow deliberation of someone who has been doing this longer than most people have been alive. Sap stains their sleeves like old tears.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Slow, measured. Each word placed like a seed. 'The willows... lean away now. They never used to lean away.'
possible_rumors: The Thicket is dying faster than it should. Something is draining the sap from beneath.
I've tended these willows for forty years. They used to lean toward the Root-Song. Now they lean away.
The Still Pool was always still. Last week, it rippled. Nothing touched it. It just... moved.

generic_templates.description: A pale figure glimpsed between the trees at dusk, tending luminescent wisps that drift through the wood like memories refusing to settle. Their lantern-staff casts no shadow.
generic_templates.voice_hint: Dreamy, distant. Answers questions with questions. 'You ask where the path leads. But have you asked where it began?'
possible_rumors: The lanterns remember where the Arborists walked. Follow the brightest one.
Some lights lead. Some lights lie. The true path hums.
I tend the lights that no one lit. Someone should ask who they were burning for.
